series_description: Report queries pulled for various work functions from COGNOS, OSCAR/AWARE, BEAMS, CATS, OAKS, OHMS, IBM, TRAC, SOPPS and any additional database accessed by the agency. These report/queries are transient in nature as they can be reproduced upon demand.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until no longer of administrative value and ; Paper: Retain until no longer of administrative value and

series_description: Records that convey information of temporary importance or contains information duplicative of official records and are of no value once received, read and/or acted upon. They do not set agency policy or establish guidelines or procedures, certify a transaction, become a receipt or hold administrative value. Records may include: unclassified background checks, information stored on electronic devices (cell phones, flash drives, other media) routine correspondence, letters, memos, announcements, transient e-mails, notes, telephone messages, meeting notes, personal notes, calendar tasks, post-it notes, notice of errors or correction, daily personnel assignments, business cards, facsimile printouts, white boards, chalk boards, marker boards, flip charts, drafts (linear, superseded & shared), copies, copier/fax activity reports, bulletins, posters, general notices, displays, blank forms, unused video/photos and any other miscellaneous record not set out in a schedule that can be described similarly.
retention_statement: Paper: May be disposed of once read or acted upon. No RC-3 is necessary. ; Electronic: May be disposed of once read or acted upon. No RC-3 is necessary.

series_description: Records generated by the agency for convenience with area business functions. Records may include information relating to staff, field offices, consultants, business contacts, agency contacts, legislative contacts, mailing lists, registries, rosters, informational directories and other information compiled either by or for the use of the agency that can be described similarly.
retention_statement: Paper: Documents are continuously updated superseded, or become obsolete. No RC-3 is necessary when destroying these records. ; Electronic: Documents are continuously updated superseded, or become obsolete. No RC-3 is necessary when destroying these records.

series_description: These records are designed to set precedence and define operational guidelines for the agency. Records may include: Policy, standard operating procedures, manuals and any additional record created similarly.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete or replaced. Once inactive, retain for five (5) calendar years and until historic review has been completed by State . An RC-3 is required when destroying these records. ; Paper: Retain until superseded, obsolete or replaced. Once inactive, retain for five (5) calendar years and until historic review has been completed by State Archives. An RC-3 is required when destroying these records.

series_description: Recording of appointments, schedules and meetings that document the function, location and/or activity of an agency employee, section or group. Records may include: calendars, appointment books, planners, organizers, itineraries and any other records not set out in a schedule that can be described similarly.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain documents for six (6) months from the recorded date or date of last entry. No RC-3 is necessary when destroying these records. ; Paper: Retain documents for six (6) months from the recorded date or date of last entry. No RC-3 is necessary when destroying these records.

series_description: Minutes generated from official meetings that document the business conducted during the meeting relating to orders, policy/procedures, directives, discussion, decisions and actions. (Examples: Commission Meetings and other committee, board, group or council meetings.)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for two (2) state fiscal years following the fiscal year created. Destroy when no longer of administrative value and after State completes historic review. An RC-3 is required when destroying these records. ; Electronic: Retain for two (2) state fiscal years following the fiscal year created. Destroy when no longer of administrative value and after State Archives completes historic review. An RC-3 is required when destroying these records.

series_description: Recording of inquiries to agency administration from the general public or government agencies. Records may include: Correspondence, responsive records and any additional record relevant to provide proper customer service to constituents.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain for three (3) state fiscal years following the date of receipt of the inquiry. Destroy when no longer of administrative value. An RC-3 is required when destroying these records. ; Electronic: Maintain for three (3) state fiscal years following the date of receipt of the inquiry. Destroy when no longer of administrative value. An RC-3 is required when destroying these records.

series_description: Record that document claims and litigation against RSC. Records may include: correspondence, court pleadings, court notices and supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for five (5) federal fiscal years from the date of final determination or when case is deemed closed. An RC-3 is required when destroying these records. ; Paper: Retain for five (5) federal fiscal years from the date of final determination or when case is deemed closed. An RC-3 is required when destroying these records.

series_description: Files that contain all records associated with appeals filed by consumers because of the provision or denial of a service. Record may include: copies of consumer case records, correspondence, letters regarding appeals, hearing records, log records, database files and supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: Appeal files are maintained in either an active or closed status. Closed consumer appeal files are maintained for three (3) federal fiscal years after the appeal is closed. An RC-3 is required when destroying these records. ; Electronic: Appeal files are maintained in either an active or closed status. Closed consumer appeal files are maintained for three (3) federal fiscal years after the appeal is closed. An RC-3 is required when destroying these records.

series_description: Records generated in connection to the Record Management Program. Record may include: Certificate of Record Disposal (RC-3); records submitted to DAS and to the State Archives; and, supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for ten (10) federal fiscal years, and ; Electronic: Retain for ten (10) federal fiscal years, and

series_description: Reports that may be created by agency staff on a scheduled (weekly, bi-weekly, monthly, annual, etc.) basis that are used individually or collectively to provide indicators on performance, growth, project success, accountability or as directed by management that are not required by law and set out in a specific retention schedule.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain for a minimum of two federal fiscal years from the date of the report. ; Electronic: Maintain for a minimum of two federal fiscal years from the date of the report.

series_description: Schedules provide a description of each record series; listing of specific records that may fall into the category and provides the time frames for retention of the records within the category.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until superseded, obsolete, or replaced. ; Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete, or replaced.

series_description: Records received or generated by the agency in relation to requests for public records. Records may include: the request, correspondence, logs, folders, responsive records, Public Record Request Log, Public Record Request Worksheet, invoices, and any other documentation relating to a request for public records.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for three (3) calendar years from the date of the request. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records. ; Electronic: Retain for three (3) calendar years from the date of the request. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records.

series_description: The Electronic Transaction Report (ETR) was mandated by the Ohio Office of Information Technology (OIT) to determine approval for agency use of electronic signatures and digital certifications. Records may include: The completed ETR form, supporting documentation and electronic environment infrastructure records.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain records while the approved process is in place and utilized by the agency. Destroy when the process becomes obsolete and no longer of administrative value. ; Electronic: Retain records while the approved process is in place and utilized by the agency. Destroy when the process becomes obsolete and no longer of administrative value.

series_description: Non-record documents that do not meet the definition of a record according to
retention_statement: Paper: Delete immediately after reading and/or responding. ; Electronic: Delete immediately after reading and/or responding.

series_description: Intermediate e-mail documents have more significant administrative, legal, and/or fiscal value but do not contain information that influences agency policy. Records may include: General/Routine Correspondence (letters, memo, referral letters, request for routine information answered by standard form letters), status reports for on-going projects and issues; and, any additional e-mail with similar content.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain one (1) calendar year. If the email becomes part of an official file (i.e. consumer file) as a result of special circumstances, the e-mail will be filed and maintained according to the file they become part of. ; Electronic: Retain one (1) calendar year. If the email becomes part of an official file (i.e. consumer file) as a result of special circumstances, the e-mail will be filed and maintained according to the file they become part of.

series_description: Badges that are issued to all employees and visitors to be used while on OOD premises to provide access to OOD buildings and for security management.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain while employed by OOD. Badges may be replaced as needed. ; Electronic: Retain while employed by OOD. Badges may be replaced as needed.

series_description: Electronic security system swipe history, demographic information and any additional information stored within the system on employees, visitors and security management.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for two (2) years from termination of employment and destroy when no longer of administrative value.

series_description: DVR recording system that is part of the security system that records designated areas on OOD premises. The system is a re-write loop with a maximum capacity.
retention_statement: Electronic: The system is a re-write loop. Recordings are overwritten continuously. Recordings are retained for a minimum of (twenty) 20 days. The re-write period may be longer than (twenty) 20 days depending on length of each motion activated recording.

series_title: Visitor Registration Log and Sign-in/out Sheets
series_description: Documents that track the arrival and departure of visitors and staff to agency offices and may document temporary badge usage. (Not used at all offices)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for one year from the date documented for the visit. ; Electronic: Retain for one year from the date documented for the visit.

series_description: The records are maintained to document the maintenance and operation of OOD vehicle fleet. Records may include monthly reports on fuel purchases, maintenance and repair invoices, Vehicle Service Record, Monthly Vehicle Progress Report, operation reports, warranty, guaranty statements, operating manual, work orders, copies of accident reports, mileage statements, use logs, and other documents or reports generated about the vehicles.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain during operation of the vehicle by the agency. Operating manuals and warranty information goes with the vehicle to State Salvage and all other records may be destroyed. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records. ; Electronic: Retain during operation of the vehicle by the agency. Operating manuals and warranty information goes with the vehicle to State Salvage and all other records may be destroyed. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records.

series_description: File containing records that list all equipment including the description, date purchased, cost, vendor, and serial number (if any) along with miscellaneous information. Records may include: Records of Transfer of Equipment and Furniture; Equipment Receipt, inventories, disposal of surplus property records; maintenance, repair and warranty repair; and any additional records relating to inventory control.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain records for the life of the equipment, plus two (2) state fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain records for the life of the equipment, plus two (2) state fiscal years.

series_description: Projected budgets prepared prior to each fiscal year at the local level to project expenditures and used to track monthly expenditures. Records may include: Request for Budget Changes; Document Changes; Information that supports budget requests; budget request FY forms used to project funds for upcoming administrative and case service funds for new fiscal year; HR budget request forms projecting funds for upcoming trainings and meetings; supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain seven (7) state fiscal years from the completion date and destroy when no longer of administrative value. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records. ; Electronic: Retain seven (7) state fiscal years from the completion date and destroy when no longer of administrative value. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records.

series_description: Master list of Audit Reports of contracts regarding OOD grants to public and private rehabilitation agencies that include information on private sector salaries and wages.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years and ; Electronic: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years and

series_description: Worksheets used for proposed budgets, legislation, testimony, and related documents.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until budget is in effect ; Electronic: Retain until budget is in effect

series_title: Social Security Reimbursement Requests & Ticket-to-Work Payment Requests
series_description: Records that document the reimbursement and payment requests for federal programs. Records may include: UI wage data, EN Split payment requests, EN payment request form, IWP Lookback tool, Social Security Reimbursement Payment Requests, SSA-199, WORK NUMBER wages and any additional record relating to these program requests.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years and ; Electronic: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years and

series_description: Federal Grant Expenditure Reports which include monthly, quarterly, annual reports and supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years and ; Electronic: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years and

series_title: Refund of Expenditure for Prior Year
series_description: Records that document receipts and payment where the original payment was made in error, duplicated, or made illegally within the biennium for the prior year.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years or longer to ensure that the documents have been audited by Auditor of State, and ; Electronic: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years or longer to ensure that the documents have been audited by Auditor of State, and

series_description: The records consist of the logs, receipts and other information provided when state credit cards are used to make purchases for supplies and other items by authorized employees. The logs provide information on vendors, purchased item, date, and supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years and ; Electronic: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years and

series_description: Records of funds deposited with the State Treasurer.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years and ; Electronic: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years and

series_description: Records used to initiate a replacement warrant when original warrant is lost or stolen. Records may include: Auditor N-53 Substitute Warrant; Auditor N-54 Duplicate; Auditor N-94 Bond of Duplicate; Auditor N-248 Affidavit of Forgery; and Auditor N-262 Rewrite of Warrant and supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years and ; Electronic: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years and

series_title: Auditor of State Re-write & Request for Stop Release Cancellation of Warrant
series_description: Documents used to change the information on the warrant and to stop the release or cancellation of the warrant.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years and ; Electronic: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years and

series_description: Journal for recording all warrants cancelled during a fiscal year. Records may include form ADM-277 used to cancel a State Warrant other than payroll; form ADM-914 is used for canceling payroll warrant only; and any supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years and ; Electronic: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years and

series_description: Treasurer of State Form 12 used to stop payment on a State warrant.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years and ; Electronic: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years and

series_description: Requests to the Controlling Board for approval of emergency expenditures for Capital Improvements, Projects, purchase made without competitive selection (Bid Waiver), transfer of Capital Improvement Funds and supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years and ; Electronic: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years and

series_description: Receipts used to deposit and record revenue from any known revenue source.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years and ; Electronic: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years and

series_description: Documents generated to obtain authorization for out of state travel of an employee.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years and ; Electronic: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years and

series_description: Awards issued by external sources honoring the Opportunities for Ohioans with Disabilities agency (OOD) as an agency (not specific staff)
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain award for as long as it is deemed to have administrative value. Review by State before disposal. Destroy when no longer of administrative value. ; Electronic: Maintain award for as long as it is deemed to have administrative value. Review by State Archives before disposal. Destroy when no longer of administrative value.

series_title: News Releases, Media Advisories, Agency Updates and Announcements
series_description: Files created to manage news releases, media advisories, event announcements and agency updates Files may include: E-mail distribution lists, e-mail confirmations, return notices, statistical data, address information, supporting documentation and any additional record generated similarly.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain for three (3) state fiscal years following the year issued. Review by State for historical value before disposal. Destroy when deemed of no value by the agency and State Archives. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records. ; Electronic: Maintain for three (3) state fiscal years following the year issued. Review by State Archives for historical value before disposal. Destroy when deemed of no value by the agency and State Archives. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records.

series_title: Accounts Payable/Receivable, Administrative Invoices, and General Ledger
series_description: Records generated in relation to accounts payable/receivable, invoices and general ledger.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years and ; Electronic: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years and

series_description: Paper files containing claimant records received by the agency during the claim determination process. Records may include confidential medical, mental health, educational, vocational related documents and supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: Files that are property of SSA but are created and maintained by OOD during the disability determination process. Once the disability determination is made the files are turned over to SSA.

series_description: Records generated during the appeal process on Social Security disability claims. Records may include: log of files and/or documents removed from the paper file to be used in the hearing process; schedules and itineraries for the hearings; hearing reports & decisions; medical records; and any additional record created or used during the hearing process.
retention_statement: Electronic: All records used during the hearing process are maintained in the paper file and are transferred to SSA upon final determination. Retain file copies until no longer of administrative value and ; Paper: All records used during the hearing process are maintained in the paper file and are transferred to SSA upon final determination. Retain file copies until no longer of administrative value and

series_description: Records generated from agency reviews of potential fraud by claimants who have applied for Social Security Disability benefits or are already receiving benefits. Records may include: Inspector General documents; primary review documents; queries; internet search documents; e-mail & correspondence; investigation reports, findings and analysis; and any additional record used or generated during the investigation process.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for five (5) federal fiscal years from the date the investigation is completed ; Electronic: Retain for five (5) federal fiscal years from the date the investigation is completed

series_description: Files containing records that are completed or provided by a medical/mental health consultant or consultant group. Files may contain contact information; licensures; forms that initiate business interactions; production data; work quality review; site visit information (for external consultants); sanction and complaint issues.
retention_statement: Paper: Contact and licensure records are updated regularly and file contains only the most current records. Files are maintained for seven (7) federal fiscal years following the last date of service to the agency. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records. ; Electronic: Contact and licensure records are updated regularly and file contains only the most current records. Files are maintained for seven (7) federal fiscal years following the last date of service to the agency. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records.

series_description: Timesheets that are completed by DDD consultants that document start and end times and contain signature of the consultant and DDD authorizing staff person.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for seven (7) federal fiscal years and ; Electronic: Retain for seven (7) federal fiscal years and

series_description: Reports generated for the Division of Disability Determination (DDD) to reflect number of workers, leaves, and overtime hours to each position category and is the timekeeping report of personnel services for disability determination services. This file includes supporting documentation used to correlate figures.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for five (5) federal fiscal years following the date of the report. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records. ; Electronic: Retain for five (5) federal fiscal years following the date of the report. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records.

series_description: Records generated during postage stamp requisitions, mailing, operating, purchase, and maintenance of mailroom equipment and supplies used to process the mail for the Agency.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain records for two (2) federal fiscal years ; Electronic: Retain records for two (2) federal fiscal years

series_description: A card completed by newly hired employees that lists demographic information by race, sex, and disability.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until information is entered in OAKS and ; Electronic: Retain until information is entered in OAKS and

series_description: File that are maintained on employees who request reasonable accommodation. Records may include: Reasonable Accommodation Employee Request; Reasonable Accommodation Supervisor’s Report; Reasonable Accommodation Treating Sources Report; correspondence; notes; purchase orders; any additional record relating to the employee’s reasonable accommodations and disposition of accommodation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for length of employment. Destroy three (3) calendar years after employment is terminated. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records. ; Electronic: Retain for length of employment. Destroy three (3) calendar years after employment is terminated. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records.

series_description: Records are maintained to provide state fiscal year analysis of projected agency expenditures with minority business vendors. Records may include: Projection Plan; Strategic Plan; Annual Minority Business Plan; Action Plan; Minority Business Enterprise Exemption Form. MBE Vendor Tracking Log and various other forms and supporting documents.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for three (3) state fiscal years from the date of approval of the strategic plan for the related state fiscal year. Review by State . A Certification of Disposal is required when destroying these records. ; Electronic: Retain for three (3) state fiscal years from the date of approval of the strategic plan for the related state fiscal year. Review by State Archives. A Certification of Disposal is required when destroying these records.

series_description: Files maintained by calendar year relating to various discrimination complaints filed by employees and; consumer complaints that fall within the agency’s jurisdiction. Record series includes documents regarding civil rights, Title VII and other discrimination complaints. Documents may include complaint form, Ohio Civil Rights Commission (OCRC) and Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC) charge affidavits filed by employees, applicants and consumers; investigation packets and, all documentation associated with the filing and settlement of these complaints.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for three (3) years from the end of the calendar year in which the case was closed. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records. ; Electronic: Retain for three (3) years from the end of the calendar year in which the case was closed. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records.

series_description: Log generated to track expenditures in relation to employee reasonable accommodations.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain a permanent version with the most current information.

series_description: Files maintained on employees containing all records associated with discipline. Records may include investigatory interviews; supporting materials; procedural documents; exhibits; notes; correspondence; Probationary Removal/Reduction Checklist; Direct Order; Copy of Notice of Disciplinary Actions; Pre-discipline Report; administrative investigation packets; incident packets; and, any other document relating to review and issuance of employee discipline.
retention_statement: Paper: Discipline is removed from this file in accordance with policy and statutory guidelines. Upon termination of employment, what is contained in the file at that time will be merged with the Personnel File and maintained according to the corresponding active schedule for Personnel Files. ; Electronic: Discipline is removed from this file in accordance with policy and statutory guidelines. Upon termination of employment, what is contained in the file at that time will be merged with the Personnel File and maintained according to the corresponding active schedule for Personnel Files.

series_description: Databases and logs created for convenience of tracking data and information associated with Labor Relations. Records may include: Discipline Database Log containing information and statistical data relating to issuance of discipline and maintains history; New Hire Orientation Log used to track completion of orientation materials from new employees; Probationary Evaluation Log used to track probationary dates; and, any other database or log created by the section for tracking purposes.
retention_statement: Paper: The most current log is maintained on a permanent basis. Destroy old log when information is updated, superceded or becomes obsolete. ; Electronic: The most current log is maintained on a permanent basis. Destroy old log when information is updated, superceded or becomes obsolete.

series_description: Records may include OOD Strike/Work Stoppage Plan for all OOD offices for management to follow along supporting documents. This report is updated every three years.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for three (3) years until superseded, deleted, or updated by new report. Destroy outdated report. ; Electronic: Retain for three (3) years until superseded, deleted, or updated by new report. Destroy outdated report.

series_description: Records may include any type of anonymous complaint received by OOD in any format in which the initial review of the complaint does not warrant an investigation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for three (3) months from the date received. ; Electronic: Retain for three (3) months from the date received.

series_description: Files maintained on employees that have filed an Unfair Labor Practice. Records may include: Complaint, Investigation Packet, State Employee Relations Board Ruling; supporting documentation and any other record generated or received by the agency in relation to the filing.
retention_statement: Paper: Records are maintained three (3) calendar years from the date the matter is resolved. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records. ; Electronic: Records are maintained three (3) calendar years from the date the matter is resolved. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records.

series_description: Packets maintained on employees who have filed a grievance under a corresponding Collective Bargaining Agreement. Records may include: Grievance Form; Hearing Officer’s Report and Recommendation; Adjudication Order; Arbitration Panel Decision; OCSEA and District 1199 Exempt Grievance Folders; step responses, exhibits, mediation, arbitration information, Non-Agreements; Due Process Meeting; Union document requests; and, any other miscellaneous record relating to the processing of a grievance.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three (3) calendar years from the date the grievance is closed. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records. (Be aware that some collective contracts specify where such documents can be kept.) ; Electronic: Retain three (3) calendar years from the date the grievance is closed. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records. (Be aware that some collective contracts specify where such documents can be kept.)

series_description: The documents in this file are compiled in a uniform format and are to be followed in an emergency situation or in case of crises. Documents are part of the current business continuity plan. Human Resource is the primary source for these records, with Facilities acting as a secondary source for incident reports and crises responses. Documents maintained in this file will include: evacuation plans; floor plans; locations for safety in case of tornado; emergency exits and extinguisher locations; fire drills; protocol for hostage situations; threatening situations domestic and other; Incident Reports; Building Evacuation Assistance Requests; crises response notes, reports, charts, documents, and correspondence.
retention_statement: Paper: The current plan is maintained on a permanent basis. Destroy old documents when a new plan is updated, superceded or becomes obsolete. ; Electronic: The current plan is maintained on a permanent basis. Destroy old documents when a new plan is updated, superceded or becomes obsolete.

series_description: Records represent Labor/Management Committee Meeting Minutes.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for two (2) calendar years from the date of the meeting.

series_description: Files are maintained by pay period on every employee to maintain agency attendance records that are not stored in the Ohio Administrative Knowledge System (OAKS). Records may include: Timesheets, bi-weekly attendance report; weather emergency documentation; leave use, conversion and accrual; overtime and compensatory time authorization; schedules and changes; activity sheets; sign-in/out sheets; time cards; change of work schedules; cost savings day declarations; call-off sheet; printouts, logs, reports, physician notes and, all other supporting documentation relating to employee attendance.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for three (3) state fiscal years from the pay period. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying this Record. ; Electronic: Retain for three (3) state fiscal years from the pay period. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying this Record.

series_description: Files maintained on employees relating to medical history, disability and FMLA. Records may include: Medical Forms, Disability Leave Cards, Application for Disability, Leave Benefits, Application for Disability Leave, Employer Statement, Supplemental Report, Physician or Health Care Provider Certification for FMLA, Disability approval and leaves, and any other record or supporting document relating to medical history, disability and FMLA.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for five (5) state fiscal years after employment terminates. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying this Record. ; Electronic: Retain for five (5) state fiscal years after employment terminates. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying this Record.

series_description: Files maintained on employees that are not U.S. Citizens that house forms issued by the Department of Homeland Security to document an employee’s ability to work in the United States. Records may include: I-9 Forms and other supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for three (3) federal fiscal years after the date of hire of employee, or one (1) year after the date the employment is terminated, whatever date is later. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying this Record. ; Electronic: Retain for three (3) federal fiscal years after the date of hire of employee, or one (1) year after the date the employment is terminated, whatever date is later. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying this Record.

series_description: Files maintained in accordance with OSHA standards on work related deaths, injuries and/or illnesses. Records may include: logs, reports, summaries and supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for five (5) calendar years from the date of incident. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying this Record. ; Electronic: Retain for five (5) calendar years from the date of incident. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying this Record.

series_description: Cards that document the FMLA approved hours of employees.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain card for one (1) calendar year from the last date entered on the card. ; Electronic: Retain card for one (1) calendar year from the last date entered on the card.

series_description: Files maintained on employees containing information and required forms for benefits and payroll processing. Records may include:, health care medical, dental and vision information, enrollment/change forms; COBRA eligibility notification; life insurance enrollment and beneficiary designation/change forms; annual affidavits of student and dependent status; Ohio Deferred Compensation forms; supplemental retirement account election forms; FBMC – Flexible Spending Account forms; leave conversion, donation and restoration forms; payroll deduction authorizations used to authorize deductions for charitable organizations, credit unions, US Savings Bonds, health insurance, or direct deposit; request for transfer of public service credit; Employers Identification Notice; state withholding exemption; out of state travel authorizations; taxpayer ID number and certification (W-9); statement of local tax liability; change of address; withholding allowance (W-4); employee suggestions; education volunteer request; telecommuting application; and, any additional supporting document relating to payroll and benefits.
retention_statement: Paper: Upon termination of employment the file will be merged with the Personnel File and maintained according to the corresponding active schedule for Personnel Files. ; Electronic: Upon termination of employment the file will be merged with the Personnel File and maintained according to the corresponding active schedule for Personnel Files.

series_description: Files created during the selection process to fill vacant positions. Records may include Position Description, Ohio Civil Service Applications, Job Application Notice, Selection Interview Questions, Application Flow Data, Narrative for Selections, Background Information & Checks, Resumes, Summary Score Sheets, Interview Score Sheets and supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for three (3) state fiscal years from the date the position is filled or decision not to fill is made. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying this record. ; Electronic: Retain for three (3) state fiscal years from the date the position is filled or decision not to fill is made. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying this record.

series_description: Chart that provides a visual view of OOD’s reporting structure and may identify staff location and supervision.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until superseded by most current information or until audited. ; Electronic: Retain until superseded by most current information or until audited.

series_description: Files maintained on temporary employees that contain required documentation and agreements with OOD. The documents may include: Confidentiality Agreements, Contracts, Timesheets and other necessary documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain records for three (3) state fiscal years from the last date work or services were performed. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying this record. ; Electronic: Retain records for three (3) state fiscal years from the last date work or services were performed. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying this record.

series_description: Files maintained on interns that contain required documentation and agreements with OOD. The documents may include: Confidentiality Agreements, Practicum/Intern Agreement, Referral, and other documentation necessary to initiate and maintain the internship.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain records for three (3) state fiscal years from the last date work or services were performed. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying this record. ; Electronic: Retain records for three (3) state fiscal years from the last date work or services were performed. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying this record.

series_description: Data tracking logs that are created for convenience to track status or functions of the section. The information contained in the logs is copied from official agency records. Logs maintained may include: Position Description Log, Job Posting Log, Supervisors’ LPC expiration dates tracking, Non- Rehabilitation Counselors’ CRC expiration tracking and Career Ladder Log.
retention_statement: Electronic: Logs are constantly updated and information becomes superseded, replaced or obsolete. Destroy when no longer of administrative value.

series_description: Records maintained on employees to track training requirements and statistical data relating to agency, policy, positions and employee’s participation in related non-credit/credit/workforce development and tuition reimbursement benefit.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for duration of employment plus two (2) months. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying this record. ; Electronic: Retain for duration of employment plus two (2) months. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying this record.

series_description: Files maintained on employees that contain copies of statewide nominations, awards and recognition by the agency as well as copies of awards generated by outside sources if a copy is provided to the training section. Records may also include those created in the selection process as well as milestone memos and certificates.
retention_statement: Paper: Original documents are provided to the employee. HR will maintain copies for two (2) calendar years from the date of the award. ; Electronic: Original documents are provided to the employee. HR will maintain copies for two (2) calendar years from the date of the award.

series_description: Records maintained to organize and track participation and agency spending on government funded (state, federal and grant awards) trainings. Records may include: monthly reconciliations by cost center, training reimbursement invoices, budget records, in-service training and non-credit/credit/workforce development records, tuition reimbursement and any addition records generated for this purpose.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for two (2) federal fiscal years ; Electronic: Retain for two (2) federal fiscal years

series_description: Administrative Records generated to assist Business Enterprise (BE) administration with oversight and tracking of the program. Records may include: Grievance Log; unassigned/assigned income/disbursement spreadsheet and supporting documentation; revenue and budget projections spreadsheets, OBG/OOD activity files; Key Bank ACH/EDI Operations Passkey; Authorizations; invoices; Equipment Audit Reports; staff meeting agendas; notes and sign-in sheets; and any addition administrative record or supporting document relating to the BE Program not already included in a retention schedule.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for three (3) federal fiscal years and ; Electronic: Retain for three (3) federal fiscal years and

series_description: The Business Enterprise Operating Program System file contains a copy of the operating system that is distributed to operators to as an optional method to produce monthly and semi-annual reports; inventories and daily cash reports as required by the Business Enterprise Program.
retention_statement: Electronic: The most current version of the software is retained on a permanent basis. Retain until the current system is updated, superseded, or becomes obsolete. Destroy outdated system.

series_description: Business Enterprise Administrative Management System (BEAMS) is an electronic tracking system designed for the Business Enterprise (BE) Program. BEAMS is used for storing, retrieving and processing data related to operators, BE staff, facilities and budgets.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain for seven (7) federal fiscal years and destroy when no longer of administrative value.

series_description: Files maintained to document the administrative activity of each facility by the Business Enterprise (BE) Program. The Administrative Facility file contains records for business continuation for the lifespan of each facility. Records may include: Bureau Grantor Agreement, contracts, permits, addendums, Memorandums of Understanding (MOU), Initial/Revised Inventory Agreement, architectural drawings, and any additional record relating to the administration of the facility.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for the life of the facility/site plus three (3) federal fiscal years from the date of closure. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records. ; Paper: Retain for the life of the facility/site plus three (3) federal fiscal years from the date of closure. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records.

series_description: Files are maintained to document the activity and business structure of each facility. The records may include: award letter, selection documentation, termination letter, vacancy announcement, complaints, annual equipment inventory, state surplus property turn-In documents, annual budget report, closing statement and invoice, Inspection Reports, monthly operating report, deductions for employers with disabilities, vehicle expense reports, OVRC/BEP reviews, grantor surveys, pictures, customer surveys, purchase and repair invoices, semi-annual stock inventory, and supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for three (3) federal fiscal years from the date of the record or until audited. ; Electronic: Retain for three (3) federal fiscal years from the date of the record or until audited.

series_description: Files maintained to document the administrative activity of each operator and records business continuation for the duration that the operator retains his/her Business Enterprise (BE) License. Records may include: BE License; BE test results; Bureau Operator Agreements; Bureau Temporary Operator Agreements; program eligibility information; signed release of information; Attorney General Certification information; BE operator''s employee job description; cleaning schedule; resignation/retirement letter or form; management or action plans; probationary actions; license terminations; license suspensions or extended leave information; active opening statement and current receipt for rules; and supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain until the license is surrendered or revoked plus three (3) federal fiscal years. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records. ; Paper: Maintain until the license is surrendered or revoked plus three (3) federal fiscal years. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records.

series_description: Files maintained to document the activity and business structure of each operator in the Business Enterprise (BE) Program. Records may include: correspondence, late/penalty letter, negative balance due letter, NSF letter, award letters, non-award letters; penalty log; OVRC nomination information; stipend invoices and information; grievance information; annual records review; annual vendors appraisal; upward mobility certificates; unassigned income invoices; and supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for at least five (5) federal fiscal years or until audited and destroy when no longer of administrative value. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records. ; Electronic: Retain for at least five (5) federal fiscal years or until audited and destroy when no longer of administrative value. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records.

series_description: Records that document the process to select an operator for a facility. Records may include: applications and supporting documentation; applicant information records provided to the selection panel; facility announcement; selection scoring form; and, other miscellaneous documentation acquired during the selection process.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain records for at least ninety (90) days from the date the Operator is chosen and accepts the facility. Destroy when no longer of administrative value. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records. ; Electronic: Maintain records for at least ninety (90) days from the date the Operator is chosen and accepts the facility. Destroy when no longer of administrative value. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records.

series_description: Records that document the election proceedings of the Ohio Vendors Representative Committee (OVRC). Records may include: completed ballots; return envelopes; ballot tally sheet; and, any additional record generated during the election and ballot review.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain documents until election results are declared and the new or re-elected member(s) have accepted the position(s). Destroy when no longer of administrative value. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records. ; Electronic: Maintain documents until election results are declared and the new or re-elected member(s) have accepted the position(s). Destroy when no longer of administrative value. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records.

series_description: The Independent Living Older Blind Program (ILOB) serves individuals who are 55 years or older and who have a significant visual impairment with the goal to maintain their independence in a non-institutional setting. Record may include: Assessment; initial interview; case notes; Consent to Obtain and Release Information Form; Examinations/Referrals; Restoration; training; Individual Living Plan (ILP); Referral to Facility; Appointment Letter; Functional Limitations Worksheet; surveys; Certificate of Eligibility; authorization and billings; vendor invoices; documentation of SD/MSD; Application for Services; reviews; and supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Electronic: Case files are maintained in an active or closed status. Active case files are retained until the status changes to closed. Closed case files are maintained for ten (10) federal fiscal years after date of closure or as required for auditing purposes and ; Paper: Case files are maintained in an active or closed status. Active case files are retained until the status changes to closed. Closed case files are maintained for ten (10) federal fiscal years after date of closure or as required for auditing purposes and

series_description: Participant case files created to document the PCA Program and continued eligibility. Records may include documentation relating to the application, evaluations, taxes, income/expenses, employment verification, job searches, education, invoices, payments, status reports, banking, facility representatives, correspondence and any additional records and supporting documentation relating to the participant's participation in the PCA Program.
retention_statement: Paper: Records are maintained in active or closed status. Active cases are retained until PCA Program eligibility is exhausted and the status changes to closed. Closed cases are destroyed ten (10) federal fiscal years from the date of closure or after any auditing requirements are met. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records. ; Electronic: Records are maintained in active or closed status. Active cases are retained until PCA Program eligibility is exhausted and the status changes to closed or after any auditing requirements are met. Closed cases are destroyed ten (10) federal fiscal years from the date of closure. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records.

series_description: Records generated in connection to accredited Community Rehabilitation Programs for services offered to Consumers and set fee allowances. Records may include: Contact lists, correspondence and any additional administrative record generated in connection to the agency’s relationship.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain one (1) federal fiscal year from the date the agreement becomes inactive. ; Electronic: Retain one (1) federal fiscal year from the date the agreement becomes inactive.

series_description: Monthly or quarterly reports that are generated by VR administration that provide an overview on case work and services to consumers. (Not to be confused with Ad Hoc reports pulled by the field for various usage). Records may include: Caseload Summary Report; Current Year Activity Report; Cooperative Agreement With BWC; Quality and Compliance Review; Management Information Report; Minority Ethnicity Report; Rehabilitation Characteristics Report; and any additional report managed in this manner by VR administration.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for two (2) federal fiscal years from the fiscal year generated. ; Electronic: Retain for two (2) federal fiscal years from the fiscal year generated.

series_description: Reports generated by VR Administration on a monthly, quarterly or annual basis that are submitted to the Federal Rehabilitation Services Administration (RSA) to provide indicators on performance. Records may include: RSA-911 Case Service Report - Annual/ RSA-113 Quarterly Cumulative Caseload Report; RSA-722 Annual Report on Appeal Process; RSA-704 State Independent Living Service annual report; RSA-2 Vocational Rehabilitation Programs/Cost Report - Annual; RSA-7-0B Independent Living Services for Older Individuals Who are blind; and any additional report generated by administration for similar purposes.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for two (2) federal fiscal years from the fiscal year generated. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records. ; Electronic: Retain for two (2) federal fiscal years from the fiscal year generated. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records.

series_description: Surveys completed by Consumers and returned to OOD anonymously. Records may include: Annual Review Survey, Post Plan Survey, Closure 26 Survey, Closure 28 Survey, Extended Employment Survey, Ineligibility Survey, and any additional consumer satisfaction surveys.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain records for two (2) calendar years from the date received by OOD. Destroy when no longer of administrative value. ; Electronic: Retain records for two (2) calendar years from the date received by OOD. Destroy when no longer of administrative value.

series_description: Participant case files contain all records received and created in relation to the participant along with case notes that document progression of the case and all actions taken on behalf of the participant. The official records are maintained electronically in the case management system. In accordance with policy, all paper records are scanned and uploaded into the electronic case record within thirty (30) days of receipt and then may be destroyed. Records may include: Application , Certificates of Eligibility, Documentation of SD/MSD, Time Extension for Eligibility, Comprehensive Assessment, Individual Plan for Employment (IPE), Time Extension for IPE, Annual Review, closure forms, school and medical records, assessment, reports from community rehabilitation providers, surveys, correspondence, and any other record received or generated similarly.
retention_statement: Paper: Case files are maintained in an active or closed status. Active case files are retained until closure of the case. Closed case files are maintained for ten (10) federal fiscal years and ; Electronic: Case files are maintained in an active or closed status. Active case files are retained until closure of the case. Closed case files are maintained for ten (10) federal fiscal years and

series_description: Participant vehicle modification case files contain all records received and created in relation to the participant along with case notes that document progression of the vehicle modification case and all actions taken on behalf of the participant. The official records are maintained electronically in the case management system. In accordance with policy, all paper records are scanned and uploaded into the electronic case record within thirty (30) days of receipt and then may be destroyed. Records may include: Application, Certificates of Eligibility, Documentation of SD/MSD, Time Extension for Eligibility, Comprehensive Assessment, Individual Plan for Employment (IPE), Time Extension for IPE, Annual Review, closure forms, school and medical records, assessment, reports for community rehabilitation providers, surveys, correspondence, and any other received or generated similarly.
retention_statement: Electronic: Closed Vehicle Modification case files are maintained for ten (10) federal fiscal years and ; Paper: Closed Vehicle Modification case files are maintained for ten (10) federal fiscal years and

series_description: Referral and demographic information stored within the electronic case management system on entities that have been referred to OOD for vocational rehabilitation services.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain information within the electronic case management system for at least six (6) month. Destroy when no longer of administrative value.

series_title: Fee Schedules for VR Medical, Psychological & Dental Services
series_description: Schedules that set the maximum fees that medical, psychological and dental service providers can charge per service to the agency.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until superseded or no longer of use to the agency. Destroy when no longer of administrative value. ; Electronic: Retain until superseded or no longer of use to the agency. Destroy when no longer of administrative value.

series_description: Reports are compiled at the end of the month or quarter for VR Administration to assist in managing fiscal resources. Records may include: Authorization Summary, Case Service Spending and vouchered reports. These reports are used as a tool to compare case spending.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for two (2) federal fiscal years from the fiscal year generated. ; Electronic: Retain for two (2) federal fiscal years from the fiscal year generated.

series_description: Records received or generated in connection with the Workforce Investment Act (WIA) and the impact on the agency. This is an ongoing act with occasional changes. Records may include: legislation, WIA Fact Sheets, Confidentiality Information, Workforce Development Department of Labor draft regulations, education, funding, Governor’s Workforce Policy Board information, LSC Study, local boards and contacts, OBES State Implementation Team, Ohio Option, Ohio Works, Performance Measurements, OOD task forces, State Plan, Transition Team documentation, training and supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: The Act is ongoing and the records may be updated, superseded or become obsolete. Destroy when no longer of administrative value. ; Electronic: The Act is ongoing and the records may be updated, superseded or become obsolete. Destroy when no longer of administrative value.

series_description: Records created to develop schedules for computer runs for software and/or database updates along with records associated with the runs. Records may include: daily schedules; run reports; run requests; and other records documenting the successful completion of a run. (Reference: DAS IT-OP-10)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until no longer of administrative or fiscal value, and ; Electronic: Retain until no longer of administrative or fiscal value, and

series_title: Cell Phone and Telephone Use Logs
series_description: Logs generated to ensure OIT Policy compliance with the use of State owned cell phones and telephones by agency staff. Records may include the Cellular Group Sign In/out Log, Record of Personal Cellular Calls Log, contractor use log and any additional log or record generated for a similar purpose.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain logs for six (6) months following the last date documented on the log and ; Electronic: Maintain logs for six (6) months following the last date documented on the log and

series_description: Records contained within the Equipment Inventory Database or on other media that tracks IT equipment, services and inventory relating to the assignment, use, service, maintenance and repairs. IT equipment and services include computers, voicemail, telephones, cell phones, copiers, faxes, scanners and any additional equipment or service managed by IT.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for one (1) state fiscal year after the year generated. Destroy when deemed to have no administrative value. ; Electronic: Retain for one (1) state fiscal year after the year generated. Destroy when deemed to have no administrative value.

series_description: Records generated to document, track and resolve user problems, account requests, equipment use and technical assistance; inventory/equipment assignment and tracking; compile monthly and annual statistics; planning reference; management analysis; collects information on the use of computer equipment for program delivery and security. (Reference: DAS IT-AS-02)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for one (1) state fiscal year after the year generated. Destroy when deemed to have no administrative value. ; Electronic: Retain for one (1) state fiscal year after the year generated. Destroy when deemed to have no administrative value.

series_description: Files created during the development, re-design or modification of any OOD network, application or system. Records may include: project management records; status reports; draft system or sub-system specifications; draft user requirements and specifications; or any additional record deemed useful within the process.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain while system is in use plus three (3) state fiscal years after the year the system goes out of service. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records. ; Paper: Maintain while system is in use plus three (3) state fiscal years after the year the system goes out of service. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records.

series_description: Files created and utilized to manage the agency’s enterprise backup procedures that serve to document all electronic records stored on agency servers used as a safeguard to restore servers in the event of an emergency or system loss. Records may include the enterprise backup schedule; backup configuration; encryption keys; system information; databases; applications; and any other record produced by the agency and stored on the server during the backup action.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain the most updated version. Destroy outdated or superseded records when no longer of administrative value.

series_description: Records generated by every agency system relating to operation of the system, servers, equipment and network devices (printers, copiers, switches, routers, etc.) to document all usage and function of the individual system. Records may include system usage files; internet usage; charge-back files; data entry logs; computer program usage; summary reports; system audits; system security; and any additional log that can be generated from an agency system.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain the most updated version. Destroy outdated or superseded records when no longer of administrative value.

series_description: Records generated for crisis management in the event of a crisis situation at an agency facility. The documents are compiled in a uniform format to be used as a plan to be followed when an emergency situation occurs.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain the most updated version. Destroy outdated or superseded records when no longer of administrative value. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records. ; Paper: Maintain the most updated version. Destroy outdated or superseded records when no longer of administrative value. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records.

series_description: Records generated by agreements and standards pertaining to OOD specific web content to document the look; feel; update frequency; tools and technology for the extranet, intranet and internet. These records are constantly updated, superseded or become obsolete. Records may include: List of standards; agency designations for committees & content providers; and any additional record generated to document the current use and status.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain the most current version. Destroy outdated or superseded records when no longer of administrative value.

series_description: Copies of agency records used as content and posted to an agency website. Website content is constantly updated, superseded and/or may become obsolete. The website serves as a media to view official agency records not the depot to store records.
retention_statement: Electronic: Website content is constantly changing. Destroy when no longer of value.

series_description: Records created during the development or modification of an automated system and are necessary to access, retrieve, manipulate and interpret data within the system. Records may include: data element dictionary, file layout, code book or table; periodic printouts from a data/database dictionary system and additional records that explain the meaning, purpose, structure, logical relationships, and origin of the data elements.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain while system is in use plus three (3) state fiscal years after the year the system goes out of service. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records.

series_description: Data generated during the creation of a master file or database used to validate a master file or database during a processing cycle. Audit files are retained to create a management audit trail to ensure the quality of data. They can aid in recreating or documenting changes in a master file or database by validating that the system is operating as it was designed (authentication). Examples of database fields used as an audit trail include: data stamp; time stamp; user identification; account number accessed.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain for seven (7) back-up cycles and destroy when no longer of administrative value.

series_description: The series consists of the Request of Employer for Separation, the employers Notice of Determination of Benefit Rights, Board of Review decision, and any other unemployment related documents.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for at least three (3) years and destroy when no longer of value. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records. ; Electronic: Retain for at least three (3) years and destroy when no longer of value. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records.

series_description: All bidding and supporting documentation including RFP’s, RFQ’s, proposals or similar documents (purchasing, construction, personnel services, etc.).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years after the bid is awarded. Destroy when no longer of administrative value. No Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records. ; Electronic: Retain for seven (7) state fiscal years after the bid is awarded. Destroy when no longer of administrative value. No Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records.

series_description: Records in this series are used to evaluate program performance of field staff to assure consistency and compliance with agency policy and Federal guidelines. Records include the following documents: Quality Assurance Case Reviews; Quality Assurance Case Review Report; Quality Assurance Case Review Form Guide; Quality Assurance Request for Administrative Review Form; Training Material; consumer surveys; project proposals; and, any additional record generated in relation to Quality Assurance.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain records for the current year plus two (2) federal fiscal years. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records. ; Electronic: Maintain records for the current year plus two (2) federal fiscal years. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records.

series_description: Administrative records relating to the organization and functions of Disability:IN Ohio. Records may include: Pamphlets, literature, fact sheets, financial funding records, bi-laws and any other miscellaneous record produced in relation to Disability:IN Ohio.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain for three (3) state fiscal years following the year in which the record was created. Destroy when no longer of administrative value or converted to Machine Readable format. A Certificate of Disposal is only required for records that have not been converted to Machine Readable format. ; Electronic: Maintain for three (3) state fiscal years following the year in which the record was created. Destroy when no longer of administrative value. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records.

series_description: Files maintained on Council members relating to their appointment and tenure on the Council. Records may include: Correspondence, meeting notes, billings, receipts, travel and per diem reports, training notes and all other records within the agency’s possession relating to specific members and appointment documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three (3) state fiscal years from the end of service, or until transferred to Machine Readable format ; Electronic: Retain three (3) state fiscal years from the end of service. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records.

series_title: Publications, Photographs, Audio & Video Files, Event Files and Consent Forms
series_description: Electronic and paper files maintained for internal (within agency) and external (public) use. Records may include: Pamphlets; magazines; newsletters; informational updates; posters; forms; postcards; documents used to submit specifications and/or encumber money for print jobs to State Printing, OPI or outside vendors; photographs, digital images, negatives, slides, transparencies; edited audio recordings and video recordings; other electronic images of people and events related to OOD; event files and any additional record created for similar purposes. This includes the consent to release of information forms.
retention_statement: Paper: Records deemed to have no agency or historic value should be retained in accordance with the agency’s transient retention schedule. Records deemed of value shall be retained for three (3) state fiscal years from the date of publication or converted to Machine Readable format ; Electronic: Records deemed to have no agency or historic value should be retained in accordance with the agency’s transient retention schedule. Records deemed of value shall be retained for three (3) state fiscal years from the date of publication or use. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying the records.

series_description: Series covers OOD postings to Social Media sites including, but not limited to Facebook, Twitter, Youtube, etc. This information includes, but is not limited to press releases, videos, events and general information about OOD. This series also covers non-OOD generated postings removed by OOD pursuant to OOD’s Policy.
retention_statement: Electronic: OOD postings - Retain until updated or no longer needed. Non-OOD postings - Retain for current month.

series_description: Training material and documents created or procured by the agency for agency sponsored trainings. Records may include: curriculums; outlines; programs; training sign-up sheets; sign-in sheets; handouts; manuals; audio-visual materials; training evaluations; and, any other record created and used for agency sponsored training.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until no longer has administrative value or superseded and/or obsolete ; Electronic: Retain until no longer has administrative value or superseded and/or obsolete. No Certificate of Record Disposal is necessary when destroying these records.

series_title: DDD Exiting Employee Personal Files Stored on SSA Servers
series_description: The Division of Disability Determination (DDD) is funded by the Social Security Administration (SSA) to make determinations of whether a claimant is disabled under Ohio law. All work done by a DDD employee is stored on an SSA server in compliance with federal law. DDD employees are designated a personal space on an SSA server to store records that are either personal in nature, transient, drafts or copies of records stored in an SSA database system. When a DDD employee leaves the agency, a copy of these files are backed up on an SSA server that is managed by OOD IT staff to ensure accessibility in case of future legal obligations.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for three (3) federal fiscal years after the employee leaves the agency. A Certificate of Disposal is required when destroying these records.
